title: A look at CSS Resets in 2018

quote.text: Computers are like Old Testament gods; lots of rules and no mercy
quote.author: Joseph Campbell

links.title: A look at CSS Resets in 2018
links.description: All browsers ship with a set of default styles that are applied to every web page in what is called the “user agent stylesheet”. A lot of styles, however, are inconsistent between the user agent stylesheets. In order to deal with the inconsistencies between user agent stylesheets, CSS resets were born.

links.title: Fast Google Fonts with Cloudflare Workers
links.description: Code walkthrough on using Cloudflare workers to improve the performance of sites using Google Fonts. The improvements can be quite dramatic and the sample provides a real-world use case of doing streaming HTML modification in a Cloudflare Worker.

links.title: Immutable Web Apps
links.description: Immutable Web Applications is a framework-agnostic methodology for building and deploying static single-page applications that: minimizes risk and complexity, simplifies and maximizes caching, minimizes the need for servers and administration of runtime environments, enables continuous delivery through simple, flexible, atomic deployments.

links.title: Reliable Webhooks Using Serverless
links.description: A great use case for serverless: how to build a reliable webhook system using SQS and Lambda.

links.title: React setState() in 10 minutes
links.description: This article is aimed at people who have already had their first approach to React, and who, as beginners, have doubts about how setState should be used.

links.title: Under the hood of React’s hooks system
links.description: Looking at the implementation and getting to know it inside out how React hooks actually work. Hooks are a powerful abstraction, but it can be very important to understand what lies behind these abstractions to be able to use hooks effectively.

links.title: The 101 course on 404 Pages
links.description: A 404 page should do more than apologize for poor navigation on behalf of your website. A properly designed 404 page could better your website’s chances of people coming back despite the inconvenience.

book.title: Web Development with Node and Express: Leveraging the JavaScript Stack
book.author: Ethan Brown
book.description: Learn how to build dynamic web applications with Express, a key component of the Node/JavaScript development stack. In this hands-on guide, author Ethan Brown teaches you the fundamentals through the development of a fictional application that exposes a public website and a RESTful API. You’ll also learn web architecture best practices to help you build single-page, multi-page, and hybrid web apps with Express.
